## Key Points {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}

**What is already known on this topic**

- Systematic reviews and meta-analyses require 100+ person-hours and a median of 67 weeks to complete
- AI tools exist for individual stages (screening, extraction, risk-of-bias assessment) but cover only 1–3 of 10+ required steps
- Manual handoffs between stages introduce transcription errors, prevent retroactive quality enforcement, and limit reproducibility to the statistical layer

**What this study adds**

- meta-pipe is the first pipeline to integrate all 10 stages of SR/MA — from topic formulation through publication-ready manuscript — with embedded quality gates at each stage boundary
- Pipeline integration reduces production time by 87–93% (14 hours vs 100+) while achieving GRADE HIGH certainty and 27/27 PRISMA compliance
- The architecture demonstrates that methodological fragmentation — not computational difficulty — is the primary barrier to reliable, reproducible evidence synthesis

**How this study might affect research, practice, or policy**

- Enables living systematic reviews by making periodic re-execution feasible
- Provides a standardized framework for cross-project comparison of evidence synthesis metrics
- Shifts the automation discourse from "which step to automate" to "how to integrate the full workflow"

:::
